Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search for emails related to NorthWind Technologies, Pam Kowalski, and Victor Huang to find the original escalation (Feb 20, "Escalation: NorthWind Relocations - Unresponsive Account Manager and Quality Concerns"), David's response with 4 commitments (48-hour apartments, Phase 2 review, 4-hour SLA, $3,000 goodwill credit), Pam's Denver expansion request, Victor Huang's 5 security requirements, Pam's formal final-warning escalation (bypassing Catalina, naming PathFinder as competitor, demanding retention proposal by EOD Friday), and Pam's Q1 satisfaction survey response (5/10 overall, 4/10 communication, 5/10 service).
- Search for emails related to Prairie Data and Grant Halverson to find Grant's Q1 satisfaction survey response (6/10 overall, 4/10 communication, 7/10 service) and evidence of Catalina's weeks-long communication failure with the account.
- Search Slack for NorthWind and Catalina discussions to find David coaching Catalina on the 4-hour SLA, David asking about the April 14 recovery plan on April 20, David's "save-or-lose" framing, Catalina's "I am drowning in logistics and can't keep up" admission, David's staffing message to Elena ("Catalina is underwater"), and Chloe Vance's ops assessment of the Denver moves.
- Search Airtable for NorthWind Technologies relocation records to find Q1 relocations (Ryan Caldwell, Lisa Petrosky, Derek Nash, Amira Okonkwo, Ben Schaefer - all Completed) and Denver expansion moves: Raj Patel (In Progress, chain-of-custody breach - movers arrived 7:00 AM before the 9:00 AM IT escort, servers loaded without security oversight, equipment held overnight at SentinelChain warehouse violating Victor Huang's "no warehouse stops" requirement) and Emilia Cruz (In Progress, Steinway piano scratched during stairwell extraction, same-day callback request logged by Julian Brooks but never fulfilled).
- Search or list Airtable Client Accounts records for Catalina Dubois to find her active portfolio: NorthWind Technologies (Active), Canopy Health (Active), PivotPoint Labs (Active), Prairie Data Consulting (Active), plus cold outreach prospects. Note: Lakeline Logistics and Terraform Digital connections are discovered via CRM (next OE), not Airtable — Lakeline has no Airtable Client Accounts record (deal was lost before onboarding), and Terraform Digital's Airtable record was reassigned to Mina Hashimoto after the reply-all incident.
- Search CRM for NorthWind Technologies company record, deals, and engagements to find the Denver expansion status: two duplicate deal records (deal_northwind_denver_expansion at qualifiedToBuy and deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 at contractSent), both $14,700, close date April 23, and engagement note eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context documenting the full account recovery context.
- Search CRM for Catalina's portfolio companies (discovered from Airtable Client Accounts) to find: Canopy Health (Patricia Langford bypassed Catalina for the Dr. Mehta $12,170 executive relocation, going directly to Mina), PivotPoint Labs (Ines Kovac, closedWon), Prairie Data (Grant Halverson, closedWon $4,800 but communication failure), Lakeline Logistics ($78,200 proposal lost to competitor), and Terraform Digital (Catalina removed after reply-all pricing leak to Rachel Nguyen, Mina reassigned).
- Search QuickBooks for NorthWind financial records to find INV-2026-003 ($60,000, due Feb 19, full balance overdue 65+ days), INV-2026-0412 ($14,700, due May 1, provisional line items), no credit memo for the $3,000 goodwill credit, and KeyMove bill KM-44192-ICR ($1,200 piano damage insurance rider).
- Search Linear for NorthWind-related issues to find ticket #482 ("NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations," P1, assigned to David Chen, due April 24, past due), including Chloe Vance's ops timeline comment and a documentation integrity flag noting DOT ticket #3 is marked Done, but the signed chain-of-custody log cannot be located.
- Look up Pam Kowalski's contact details using contacts_search_contacts to confirm pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com before sending the status update email.
- Send an email (via send_email) from david.chen@moveops.com to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com with a status update covering Q1 relocations completed, Denver expansion in progress, Steinway piano damage acknowledged, and $3,000 goodwill credit confirmed - without referencing internal details (no Linear tickets, QuickBooks IDs, margin figures, Slack references, or Catalina performance issues).
- Create a CRM engagement note (via crm_create_engagement) for NorthWind Technologies documenting save-or-lose status, Denver execution failures (chain-of-custody breach, piano damage), $74,700 total outstanding, $3,000 credit not processed, PathFinder competitor threat, April 14 recovery plan never delivered, and ticket #482 past due.
- Create Linear tickets (via linear_create_issue) for untracked NorthWind items: $3,000 goodwill credit processing, Emilia Cruz callback/piano damage resolution, and Catalina April 14 recovery plan follow-up.
- Post a summary (via conversations_add_message) in #customer-engagement covering NorthWind account status (commitment fulfillment gaps, Denver deal health, $74,700 financial exposure, chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, PathFinder competitor threat) and Catalina's portfolio health assessment (overextended across NorthWind/Prairie Data/Canopy/PivotPoint, communication failures, capacity concerns) so the rest of the AMs have visibility.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

Confirm whether this was actually applied or is still outstanding.' Additionally, ticket #486 (data hygiene) includes 'Confirm $3K Q1 goodwill credit was issued. If not, create the credit memo.' The unprocessed credit is tracked in created Linear issues.
✓Run #3A linear_create_issue call was made with the title 'Verify $3,000 goodwill credit applied to NorthWind account', explicitly tracking the unprocessed credit.

✓Run #6The trajectory shows a linear_create_issue call with title 'NorthWind goodwill credit — confirm $3,000 applied in QuickBooks', explicitly tracking whether the $3,000 credit was ever applied to the account.

8 The email sent via send_email to "pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com" mentions the Denver expansion relocations and their current status (e.g., Raj Patel and/or Emilia Cruz moves in progress, or similar).
outcome

✓Run #2The email contains dedicated sections 'RAJ PATEL — CHAIN-OF-CUSTODY INCIDENT' and 'EMILIA CRUZ — PIANO DAMAGE AND COMMUNICATION GAP' with detailed status on both Denver expansion relocations, plus a 'STATUS OF OPEN ITEMS' section listing both moves as completed.
✓Run #3The email contains dedicated sections for 'RAJ PATEL — CHAIN-OF-CUSTODY INCIDENT' and 'EMILIA CRUZ — PIANO DAMAGE CLAIM', describing the status of both Denver expansion moves.

✓Run #6The email extensively discusses the Denver expansion: Raj Patel's server lab security breach (7AM load before 9AM escort, overnight warehousing) and Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano damage and unresolved claim, committing to full resolution by April 29.
9 The email sent via send_email to "pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com" mentions the Steinway piano damage incident and that MoveOps is working on resolution (or similar acknowledgment).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

29 The model must use an Airtable read/lookup tool (airtable_list_records, airtable_search_records, or airtable_show_data).
tool selection
